name: France
notes: Catch-all for wines without specific regional designation

name: Bordeaux
notes: Major wine region, Left/Right Bank and Entre-Deux-Mers sub-regions

name: Chablis
notes: Northernmost Burgundy, Chardonnay on Kimmeridgian limestone

name: Côte de Nuits
notes: Grand Cru Pinot Noir: Gevrey-Chambertin, Vosne-Romanée, etc.

name: Côte de Beaune
notes: Grand Cru Chardonnay: Meursault, Puligny-Montrachet, etc.

name: Alsace
notes: Aromatic whites: Riesling, Gewurztraminer, Pinot Gris, Muscat

name: The Rhône Valley
notes: Syrah in north, Grenache blends in south

name: Languedoc
notes: France's largest wine region by volume

name: The Dordogne and South West France
notes: Bergerac, Monbazillac, Cahors, Madiran, Jurançon, Côtes de Gascogne

name: Germany
notes: Catch-all for wines without specific regional designation

name: Rheingau
notes: Anbaugebiet. South-facing Rhine slopes, premium Riesling

name: Württemberg
notes: Anbaugebiet. Red wine focus, Trollinger, Lemberger

name: Italy
notes: Catch-all for wines without specific regional designation

name: Trentino-Alto Adige
notes: Alpine, Germanic influence in Alto Adige

name: Tuscany
notes: Chianti, Brunello, Vino Nobile, Bolgheri, Super Tuscans

name: Campania
notes: Taurasi, Fiano di Avellino, Greco di Tufo

name: Langhe
notes: Barolo, Barbaresco, Dolcetto d'Alba, Nebbiolo d'Alba — prestige Nebbiolo zone

name: Monferrato
notes: Barbera d'Asti, Grignolino — distinct terroir east of Langhe

name: Roero
notes: Roero Arneis DOCG, Roero DOCG — distinct sandy soils between Langhe and Monferrato

name: Chianti
notes: Chianti DOCG + Chianti Classico DOCG — Tuscany's Sangiovese heartland

name: Montalcino
notes: Brunello di Montalcino DOCG — world-class Sangiovese

name: Montepulciano
notes: Vino Nobile di Montepulciano DOCG — Sangiovese (Prugnolo Gentile) at altitude

name: Bolgheri
notes: Super Tuscan territory — Sassicaia, Ornellaia, coastal Cab/Merlot

name: Valpolicella
notes: Amarone, Ripasso, Corvina/Rondinella — one of Italy's most famous zones

name: Soave
notes: Major white wine zone — Garganega on volcanic soils

name: Etna
source: WSET L3 spec p.13, Oxford Companion
notes: Volcanic terroir — Nerello Mascalese, Carricante, distinct modern identity

name: Franciacorta
notes: Italy's premier traditional method sparkling DOCG

name: Spain
notes: Catch-all for wines without specific regional designation

name: The North West
notes: Rías Baixas, Valdeorras, Bierzo — Albariño, Godello, Mencía

name: Castilla-La Mancha
notes: La Mancha, Valdepeñas — Spain's central plateau

name: Castilla y León
notes: VdlT designation for regional wines. Geographically overlaps The Duero Valley — DOs (Ribera del Duero, Toro, Rueda) map to Duero Valley, VdlT wines map here.

name: Rioja
notes: DOCa — Spain's most famous wine region, Tempranillo-dominant

name: Navarra
notes: DO — distinct identity, rosados and reds, Garnacha

name: Penedès
notes: DO — Cava heartland, diverse still wines

name: Priorat
notes: DOCa — one of only 2 DOCas, old vine Garnacha/Cariñena on llicorella slate

name: Ribera del Duero
notes: DO — major red region, Tinto Fino (Tempranillo) at altitude

name: Toro
notes: DO — powerful reds, Tinta de Toro (Tempranillo) from old vines

name: Bierzo
notes: DO — Mencía, rising star, slate soils

name: La Mancha
notes: DO — largest single DO in the world by area

name: Valencia
notes: DO — large and diverse, Mediterranean coast

name: Jerez
notes: Sherry country — Fino, Manzanilla, Oloroso, Amontillado, Palo Cortado, PX

name: Sonoma County
notes: Diverse varieties, cool-climate Pinot Noir and Chardonnay

name: Monterey
notes: Wind-cooled Salinas Valley, Chardonnay, Pinot Noir

name: Santa Barbara County
notes: Sta. Rita Hills, Santa Ynez Valley — Pinot Noir, Chardonnay

name: Columbia Valley
notes: Vast AVA, Cabernet Sauvignon, Merlot, Syrah

name: Western Cape
notes: WO geographical unit. Contains Coastal Region, Breede River Valley, Cape South Coast in WO hierarchy, but modeled as L1 peer. For multi-region blends and wines without a more specific WO designation.

notes: Historic wine ward, cool maritime climate

name: Durbanville
notes: Cool, elevated ward near Cape Town

name: Worcester
notes: Large district, brandy and bulk wine
